title: How the 1-to-1 camp model helps campers participate more fully
excerpt: A closer look at why volunteer support matters so much in a camp environment built for children and young adults with disabilities.

sections.heading: Support changes the feeling of camp
sections.body: The value of a 1-to-1 support model is not only practical. It helps campers move through camp with more confidence, more personal attention, and more opportunity to participate in the daily rhythm of camp life.

sections.heading: Volunteers create relationship, not just supervision
sections.body: When counselors walk closely with campers through meals, activities, worship, recreation, and cabin life, camp feels more relational and less transactional. That difference can shape the whole experience for campers and families.

sections.heading: Families often feel the impact before camp even starts
sections.body: Knowing there is thoughtful volunteer support in place can reduce anxiety and help families trust that camp is prepared to welcome their child with care.

cta.eyebrow: See the role behind the model
cta.heading: Read the volunteer overview if you want to understand how counselors, junior counselors, and medical staff make the camp experience possible.
cta.body: The public site now keeps those support pathways close to the stories that explain why they matter.
